title: License-verification gate / approved-source checker ("unknown = excluded")
context: License compliance is a hard requirement for open-flashcards. Cards sourced from non-open material expose the project to legal risk and undermine its integrity. The 'unknown = excluded' rule must be enforced automatically at CI time so no card with an unverified or non-approved source can ever be published. This gate is the automated implementation of the research-002 approved-source list and rules.
objective: Build an automated license-verification gate that checks every card's source against the approved-source list, enforces the 'unknown = excluded' rule, flags share-alike/NC constraints, and runs as a CI check.
acceptanceCriteria: Rejects any card whose source is not on the approved-source list or whose license is not verifiably PD/CC-* ('unknown = excluded').
Records SPDX-style license identifier, attribution string, source URL, and retrieval date per accepted card.
Flags share-alike (CC-BY-SA) or non-commercial (NC) sources that would constrain or block the deck's intended output license.
Runs as a CI check alongside the deck linter; emits an auditable report with no secrets in logs.
Approved-source list is stored as a versioned config file (approved-sources.json) that can be updated without changing tool code.
output: A merged PR adding a license-verification CLI tool (tools/check-licenses/) with an approved-sources config and a CI workflow integration; licensed MIT.
